mentions.raw_name: Lord Beric Dondarrion (the Lightning Lord, Lord of Corpses)
Ghost of High Heart (unnamed dwarf woman)
Ser Arthur Dayne (the Sword of the Morning, referenced)
Gregor Clegane (the Mountain That Rides, referenced)
Ghost (Jon's direwolf, referenced by comparison — "red as the eyes of Jon's wolf")
House Clegane (three black dogs on yellow field)
R'hllor / the Lord of Light (Thoros's god; flame visions; "the night is dark and full of terrors")
Old Gods (weirwoods at High Heart; ghost says they "linger here"; "they remember when the First Men came with fire in their fists")
Faith of the Seven (referenced as "seven-besotted Westeros")
Dornishmen (Edric Dayne; Arya's expectations vs. reality of Dornish appearance)
First Men (ghost references them coming "with fire in their fists")
Ironborn ("iron squids turn on one another")
"Jenny's song" (also called "My Jenny's song" by the ghost; Tom sings it as payment)

mentions.raw_name: Flame visions / fire-gazing (Thoros sees past, future, and distant events in flames; sees Lannisters besieging Riverrun)
Resurrection (Lord Beric has been brought back six times by Thoros; "fire consumes")
Ghost of High Heart's prophetic dreams (she "dreamt him dead and he died"; multiple cryptic visions)
Weirwood communion (the weirwoods whisper in the ghost's ear when she sleeps; old gods linger at High Heart)
Death-sensing (ghost smells death on Lord Beric and on Arya)
Robert's Rebellion (Harrenhal tourney during "the year of the false spring"; the Trident; Goodbrook's village burned by Hoster Tully)
War of the Five Kings (ongoing; Lannisters moving on Riverrun; ironborn turning on each other)
Battle of the Mummer's Ford (Ned Dayne saved Beric from drowning there)
The Sword of the Morning (Ser Arthur Dayne)
Lord of Corpses (ghost's name for Beric Dondarrion)
Hand of the King (implied — "the Hand's tourney")
Lord Commander (implied via Night's Watch reference)
King (multiple — Robert, Aerys, Balon Greyjoy as "kraken king")
